mcp_tool_logs.observation: Tool calls captured in LLM logs but not in dedicated MCP log table. This suggests the Claude Code session's tool calls were proxied but not routed through Bifrost's MCP gateway pipeline.

features.name: MCP Catalog (add servers)
features.evidence: Visible in Web UI sidebar under MCP Gateway

features.name: Auth Config (tool-level)
features.evidence: Behind enterprise paywall in Web UI

features.name: Per-tool observability
features.evidence: Unknown — need to test with MCP server configured

features.name: Tool call approval flow
features.evidence: Manual mode = LLM suggests, app approves

features.name: Federated MCP (API→MCP)
features.evidence: Listed as enterprise feature in research

observations.finding: Free tier has 8 MCP features — enough to be genuinely useful
observations.impact: Developers will actually adopt the MCP gateway, not just try it

observations.finding: No MCP tool calls in dedicated log table despite tool_calls in LLM logs
observations.impact: The free MCP experience may not feel differentiated from basic proxying. If tool calls only appear in LLM logs (not a dedicated MCP view), the governance narrative doesn't start building.

observations.finding: Enterprise features show 'Book a demo' paywall in Web UI
observations.impact: The paywall exists but doesn't explain WHY governance matters. A developer who hasn't felt the pain of uncontrolled tool access won't be motivated to 'Book a demo'. The free tier should surface the governance gap (e.g., 'X tool calls had no authorization policy') before presenting the gate.
